description: You are a tutor preparing an interactive polar curve gallery in GeoGebra for a Calculus II student who struggles to visualize polar equations. Create a construction that includes a three-petal rose ($r = \sin(3\theta)$), a cardioid ($r = 1 + \cos(\theta)$), and a parameterized rose ($r = \sin(n\theta)$) controlled by a slider named 'n' with a range of 1 to 8 and an increment of 1. Add text annotations identifying the curves, such as "Three-petal rose: r = sin(3θ)" and "Cardioid: r = 1 + cos(θ)". Save the construction as polar_curves.ggb in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
